perturbation.original_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon the Parties with respect to any Confidential Information (a) that was possessed by the receiving party before receipt; (b) is, or becomes a matter of, public knowledge through no fault of the receiving party; (c) is rightfully received from a third party not owing a duty of confidentiality to the disclosing party; (d) is disclosed without a duty of confidentiality to a third party by, or with the authorization of, the disclosing party; or (e) is independently developed.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon the Parties with respect to any Confidential Information (a) that was possessed by the receiving party before receipt; (b) is, or becomes a matter of, public knowledge through no fault of the receiving party; (c) is rightfully received from a third party not owing a duty of confidentiality to the disclosing party; (d) is disclosed without a duty of confidentiality to a third party by, or with the authorization of, the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause '(e) is independently developed' creates an in-text contradiction. Now, if a party independently develops the same information they received confidentially, they are still bound by the NDA, even though they arrived at the information independently. This is contradictory to standard NDA practice.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall not be construed as creating, conveying, transferring, granting or conferring upon either party any rights, license or authority in or to the information exchanged, except the limited right to use Confidential Information specified in Section 2 above. Furthermore and specifically, no license or conveyance of any intellectual property rights is granted or implied by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The omission of clause (e) 'is independently developed' from the definition of Confidential Information directly contradicts the clause stating that the agreement does not grant any rights or licenses to the information exchanged, except the limited right to use Confidential Information specified in Section 2. With the omission, a party who independently develops the information would still be bound by the confidentiality agreement, even though the other clause suggests they should have rights to use independently created information.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of a breach, or threatened breach of any provision of this Agreement, the non-breaching party shall give the breaching party a notice to cure within three (3) business days. In the event the breach is not cured, the non-breaching party shall have the right to bring appropriate SPA DE SOLEIL, INC.​ ® MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT PAGE 2 v.20171020 legal action in the appropriate State or Federal court as set forth in Paragraph 9 above. The non-breaching party shall have the right to bring a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction and permanent injunction in addition to receiving any damages which the non-breaching party may prove in the appropriate litigation identified in Paragraph 9 above.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a breach of any provision of this Agreement, the breaching party will be liable for direct damages only.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'notice to cure' and the right to seek injunctive relief, the changed text now limits the remedies available to the non-breaching party solely to direct damages. This contradicts the initial intention of protecting confidential information quickly (through injunctions) and allows a breaching party to continue disclosing, so long as they can pay damages, contradicting paragraphs 9 and 10 by severely limiting remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The validity, construction and performance of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws, without regard to the laws as choice or conflict of laws, of the state of California. In the event of any dispute involving this Agreement, the parties hereby agree that such dispute shall be resolved in the appropriate State or Federal court, in the county of Los Angeles, state of California, and the parties hereby agree that proper venue and jurisdiction shall lie with any court of competent jurisdiction in the county of Los Angeles, state of California.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the remedies available to the non-breaching party to direct damages only, removing the right to seek injunctive relief. The contradicted text specifies that disputes will be resolved in the appropriate State or Federal court in Los Angeles. The original text allowed for temporary restraining orders, preliminary injunctions, and permanent injunctions. By limiting remedies to direct damages, the changed text contradicts the original agreement's intention to provide a full range of legal options, creating a significant in-text contradiction because it affects the enforceability and remedies within the court system specified in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Binding Arbitration Provision: This paragraph is inserted for the purposes of electing binding arbitration which will prevail over the provisions in Paragraphs 9 and 10 with respect to resolution of any dispute. WWWIn order to have this Paragraph 11 in effect, upon execution of this Agreement by both parties in the signature block on Page 5, both parties must sign at the bottom of said Paragraph 11 for this Paragraph 11 to be binding. If both parties do not sign this Paragraph 11 then the provisions of Paragraphs 9 and 10 shall control. If both parties sign below in Paragraph 11 then the provisions of this paragraph with respect to conducting any dispute shall control over the litigation provisions provided in Paragraphs 9 and 10. However, the laws of California shall apply and the arbitration shall take place in Los Angeles, California as set forth below. Upon signing at the bottom of Paragraph 11 in addition to signing on Page 5, the parties agree to submit to binding arbitration in the state of California which shall be a binding arbitration proceeded to be conducted under the Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association of Los Angeles, California. The subject to binding arbitration will include, without limitation, disputes regarding any breach of contract by either party or any other remedies available to a non-breaching party for failure to perform a condition of this Agreement. The parties agree that such binding arbitration shall be a final decision of the American Arbitration Association. The parties agree that arbitration will be the sole means of resolving such disputes and both parties waive any rights to resolve disputes by a court proceeding or other means and any judgment received in such binding arbitration shall be enforced in the appropriate State or Federal court. In the event of a breach of this Agreement, it is specifically agreed that the non-breaching party may bring a cause of action for a preliminary and permanent injunction in the appropriate state or federal court and thereafter submit the balance of the dispute to binding arbitration if elected under this section. Each party shall pay for one-half the cost of the arbitration. The prevailing party shall be reimbursed for said costs from the non- prevailing party. We specifically elect the arbitration provision in this Agreement: Party 1 : Spa de Soleil Party 2: Business
perturbation.changed_text: Binding Arbitration Provision: The parties agree to resolve all disputes through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the explicit requirement for both parties to sign the arbitration clause to make it effective and the provision for injunctive relief creates an in-text contradiction. With the changed text, all disputes *must* go to arbitration, eliminating the choice outlined in the original Paragraph 11 regarding the election to arbitrate by signing, contradicting the original framework. It now automatically enforces arbitration regardless of intent to sign it or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Binding Arbitration Provision: The parties agree to resolve all disputes through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association. 12. All notices hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been duly given if delivered personally, one day after delivery to a nationally recognized overnight delivery service, charges prepaid, or three days after being sent by registered or certified mail, postage prepaid, to the parties at their respective addresses set forth above and:
perturbation.justification: The original text outlines a specific process for electing binding arbitration, requiring signatures from both parties to activate the clause and allowing for injunctive relief in certain circumstances. The changed text removes this election process and automatically mandates binding arbitration for all disputes. This directly contradicts the original framework of Paragraph 11, where arbitration was contingent on both parties signing the agreement. Additionally, it appears to remove the possibility of injunctive relief, which was previously permitted. The contradicted text 'The parties agree to resolve all disputes through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association' highlights the complete removal of the conditional election of arbitration, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties concerning the disclosure of Confidential Information and supersedes any prior agreements, understandings, or representations with respect thereto. Any addition or modification to this Agreement must be made in writing and signed by authorized representatives of both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties concerning the disclosure of Confidential Information and supersedes any prior agreements, understandings, or representations with respect thereto. Any addition or modification to this Agreement can be made verbally between both parties.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the requirement that modifications must be in writing and signed to allowing verbal modifications, the clause is directly contradicting itself and legal best practices. The modified text now states, at first, that the agreement supersedes *any* prior agreements and is the *entire understanding* of both parties, and then goes on to say that the agreement can be modified verbally. It is an internal contradiction to the standard 'entire agreement' clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties concerning the disclosure of Confidential Information and supersedes any prior agreements, understandings, or representations with respect thereto. Any addition or modification to this Agreement can be made verbally between both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies a clause that originally required all modifications to the agreement to be in writing and signed by both parties to allow for verbal modifications. This directly contradicts the 'entire understanding' clause, which states that the agreement supersedes all prior agreements and understandings. This is an in-text contradiction because the agreement now simultaneously asserts that it is the complete understanding and that it can be altered verbally, creating ambiguity and unenforceability.
